div.p: This dashboard answers the primary rollout questions: which hosts upgraded successfully, which hosts failed, which hosts are not on the target UF version, what UF versions are currently deployed, and whether retry prevention, retry control, or installer cleanup issues were observed.

High-level view of upgrade attempts, successful upgrades, failed upgrades, current target version coverage, retry prevention, retry control activity, state tracking errors, and installer cleanup status.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ dc(host) AS "Total Hosts Attempted" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9812, 9813) AND host IN ($hostname$)

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ dc(host) AS "Successful Upgrades" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9812) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Success AND host IN ($hostname$)

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ dc(host) AS "Failed Upgrades" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9811, 9813) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Failure AND host IN ($hostname$)

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_internal WHERE (source IN (*metrics.log*) AND splunk_internal.group IN (tcpin_connections) AND splunk_internal.os=Windows AND hostname IN ($hostname$)) BY hostname, splunk_internal.version, _time | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(splunk_internal.version) AS uf_version by hostname | stats count AS total_hosts count(eval(uf_version = "$target_version$")) AS target_hosts | eval "Target Version Coverage"=if(total_hosts > 0, round((target_hosts/total_hosts)*100, 2), 0) | fields "Target Version Coverage"

single.title: Retry Prevention Events

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Retry Prevention Events" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode=9819 AND host IN ($hostname$)

single.option: Same MSI skipped to prevent retry loop

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Retry Control Actions" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9820, 9830, 9833) AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "State Tracking Errors" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode=9822 AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Installer Cleanup Failures" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode=9807 AND host IN ($hostname$)
| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count AS "Installer Cleanup Success" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode=9823 AND host IN ($hostname$)

div.p: Host-level upgrade outcome tables showing which systems completed successfully and which systems reported failed upgrade attempts.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ latest(_time) AS latest_time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.date) AS Date latest(splunk_upgrade_events.time) AS Time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode) AS EventCode latest(splunk_upgrade_events.status) AS Status latest(splunk_upgrade_events.message) AS Message latest(splunk_upgrade_events.previous_version) AS "Previous UF Version" latest(splunk_upgrade_events.current_version) AS "Current UF Version" FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9812) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Success AND host IN ($hostname$) BY host | rename host AS Hostname | eval "Latest Event Time"=strftime(latest_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | table Date, Time, Hostname, "Previous UF Version", "Current UF Version", EventCode, Status, Message, "Latest Event Time" | sort 0 Hostname

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ latest(_time) AS latest_time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.date) AS Date latest(splunk_upgrade_events.time) AS Time latest(splunk_upgrade_events.current_version) AS "Current UF Version" latest(splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode) AS EventCode latest(splunk_upgrade_events.status) AS Status latest(splunk_upgrade_events.message) AS Message latest(splunk_upgrade_events.details) AS Details FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_upgrade_events WHERE splunk_upgrade_events.eventcode IN (9811, 9813) AND splunk_upgrade_events.status=Failure AND host IN ($hostname$) BY host | rename host AS Hostname | eval "Latest Event Time"=strftime(latest_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | table Date, Time, Hostname, "Current UF Version", EventCode, Status, Message, Details, "Latest Event Time" | sort 0 Hostname

div.p: Current Universal Forwarder version distribution based on recent internal forwarder connection data from metrics.log.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_internal WHERE (source IN (*metrics.log*) AND splunk_internal.group IN (tcpin_connections) AND splunk_internal.os=Windows AND hostname IN ($hostname$)) BY hostname, splunk_internal.version, _time, splunk_internal.sourceHost, splunk_internal.os | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(splunk_internal.version) AS "UF Version" latest(splunk_internal.os) AS OS by hostname | stats count AS "Host Count" by "UF Version" | sort -"Host Count"

| tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_internal WHERE (source IN (*metrics.log*) AND splunk_internal.group IN (tcpin_connections) AND splunk_internal.os=Windows AND hostname IN ($hostname$)) BY hostname, splunk_internal.version, _time, splunk_internal.sourceHost, splunk_internal.os | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(splunk_internal.version) AS "UF Version" latest(splunk_internal.os) AS OS latest(splunk_internal.sourceHost) AS "Source IP" by hostname | rename hostname AS Hostname | eval "Last Seen"=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | eval "Target Status"=if('UF Version'="$target_version$", "On Target", "Not on Target") | table Hostname, "UF Version", "Target Status", OS, "Source IP", "Last Seen" | sort 0 Hostname

div.p: Windows Universal Forwarders that are not currently reporting the configured target version.

search.query: | tstats summariesonly=$DM_Accel$ count FROM datamodel=splunk_upgrade.splunk_internal WHERE (source IN (*metrics.log*) AND splunk_internal.group IN (tcpin_connections) AND splunk_internal.os=Windows AND hostname IN ($hostname$)) BY hostname, splunk_internal.version, _time, splunk_internal.sourceHost, splunk_internal.os | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(splunk_internal.version) AS "UF Version" latest(splunk_internal.os) AS OS latest(splunk_internal.sourceHost) AS "Source IP" by hostname | where 'UF Version'!="$target_version$" | rename hostname AS Hostname | eval "Target Version"="$target_version$" | eval "Last Seen"=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | table Hostname, "UF Version", "Target Version", OS, "Source IP", "Last Seen" | sort 0 Hostname
